source.description: A six-panel raster contact sheet generated for the locked July 9 Sketcha.day slug, then cropped into step frames and the finished master.
source.notes: The sheet keeps one round sugar cookie on the same folded napkin, with icing and crumb details introduced before the final.

process_strategy: The cookie and napkin are built from simple circles, folded cloth angles, scalloped edge, icing patch, crumbs, and warm tone before the final only darkens keeper lines and deepens existing shading.

final_elements.name: round cookie body
final_elements.description: A slightly imperfect round sugar cookie seen from above at a gentle angle.

final_elements.name: folded napkin base
final_elements.description: A simple folded napkin diamond sitting under the cookie.

final_elements.name: scalloped cookie edge
final_elements.description: Small uneven bumps around the cookie edge.

final_elements.name: napkin folds and cast shadow
final_elements.description: Fold lines on the napkin and a soft shadow under the cookie.

final_elements.name: icing patch
final_elements.description: A simple irregular icing area on top of the cookie.

final_elements.name: crumbs and sugar dots
final_elements.description: Small crumb marks around the cookie and tiny sugar dots on the top.

final_elements.name: warm cookie tone
final_elements.description: Restrained golden colored-pencil tint on the cookie.

frames.visible_job: Draws the round cookie guide over a simple folded napkin shape.
Turns the smooth cookie guide into a slightly scalloped baked edge.
Adds napkin fold lines and the first cast shadow under the cookie.
Adds an irregular icing patch on top of the already placed cookie.
Adds crumb marks, sugar dots, warm cookie tint, and deeper existing napkin shadow.

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens the keeper contours, clarifies the existing icing, crumbs, sugar dots, napkin folds, warm tint, and cast shadow.
allowed_changes: small cleanup marks on existing cookie and napkin details

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
